title: PM Jan Dhan Yojana
meta_description: PM Jan Dhan Yojana provides zero-balance bank accounts with RuPay debit card and ₹2 lakh accident insurance for all Indians.

hero.one_liner: Zero-balance savings account with RuPay debit card, ₹2 lakh accident insurance, and overdraft facility

what_is_it: PM Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY) launched on 28 August 2014 is the world's largest financial inclusion program. It gives every unbanked Indian a basic savings account at zero balance, a RuPay debit card, and built-in accident insurance of ₹2 lakh.

Over 52 crore accounts have been opened with total deposits exceeding ₹2.3 lakh crore. These accounts are the primary channel for receiving Direct Benefit Transfers (DBT) from schemes like PM Kisan, LPG subsidy, MNREGA wages, and scholarships.

eligibility.label: Who can open
eligibility.value: Any Indian citizen without a bank account

eligibility.label: Documents
eligibility.value: Aadhaar card (or voter ID/driving license + address proof)

eligibility.label: No documents?
eligibility.value: Small account can be opened with self-certification

how_to_apply.title: Visit any bank branch
how_to_apply.description: Carry Aadhaar card. Any nationalized bank, SBI, or private bank must open this account.

how_to_apply.title: Fill the account opening form
how_to_apply.description: Ask for Jan Dhan form. Fill basic details. Bank cannot refuse this account.

how_to_apply.title: Get RuPay card and passbook
how_to_apply.description: Receive RuPay debit card (ATM + purchases) and passbook. ₹2 lakh accident insurance auto-linked.

important_alert.text: Banks cannot refuse to open a Jan Dhan account. If refused, complain to Banking Ombudsman or Lead District Manager.

faqs.question: Is there any charge?
faqs.answer: No. Account opening is free, no minimum balance, no annual maintenance charges for basic account.

faqs.question: Can I receive government subsidies here?
faqs.answer: Yes. PM Kisan, LPG subsidy, MNREGA wages, scholarships — all DBT payments can come to this account.

faqs.question: What about overdraft facility?
faqs.answer: Eligible account holders can get overdraft up to ₹10,000. This is available after 6 months of satisfactory account operation.
